Hi @yue-fred-gao  @lolyu , could you please help to review the HLD and related PRs?
/azp run
Azure Pipelines successfully started running 1 pipeline(s).

> ### 📊 Overview
> **Files Changed**: 1 (`docs/HLD/vpp-inner-aware-flow-hash.md`) **Lines**: +860
> 
> HLD-only PR documenting the inner-aware flow hash feature for SONiC-VPP. The document is well-structured, technically precise, and covers the design space thoroughly.
> 
> ### ✅ Strengths
> * **Two-surface architecture** (IP-layer opt-in + LAG always-on with fallback) is clearly motivated and diagrammed — the ASCII packet-flow diagrams in §4.2 make the separation immediately obvious.
> * **§4.4 LAG rationale is solid.** The ABI/CRC compatibility argument against introducing a new `BOND_API_LB_ALGO` enumerator is correct and well-explained. Adding a new enum value would require regenerating libsaivs against the patched VPP headers and bumping the sonic-buildimage submodule _before_ any deployment — the `always-on with safe fallback` approach avoids all of that while being a strict generalisation of the prior behaviour (non-tunnel packets are unaffected). I read the concern raised in the inline thread, and the HLD's §4.4 counter-argument holds: the "new algo" path would impose an ABI and deployment dependency that the always-on-with-fallback path cleanly avoids.
> * **§4.5 What Is Not Hashed** is a good addition — VxLAN, Geneve, fragmented outers, ESP, and NVGRE VLAN-tagged inner are all documented.
> * **End-to-end test results** (16/16 pass on vlab-vpp-01) and **§5.2 DUT verification commands** are exactly what a hardware operator needs to validate the deployment.
> * **Appendix B** (VPP Flow-Hash + Hash Function API Reference) is a nice addition that will outlive this PR as a reference.
> 
> ### 📝 Review Findings
> #### 📝 Minor Issues
> **SRv6 missing from §2 Scope exclusions and §4.5 What Is Not Hashed** (also flagged inline by yue-fred-gao on line 33)
> 
> SRv6 uses the IPv6 Routing Extension Header (Next Header = 43, SRH Type 4). The current implementation's outer-IPv6 extension-header walker in `ip6_compute_flow_hash` only handles HBH (0) and Fragment (44) headers — it does not walk past an SRH. As a result:
> 
> * An SRv6-encapsulated packet will fall through to the outer-only hash (protocol 43 is neither IP-in-IP nor GRE), which is safe but means the inner flow entropy is not used.
> * This is the correct safe-fallback behaviour, but a reader might wonder whether SRv6 inner peek is intentionally excluded or simply an oversight.
> 
> Recommended fix: add SRv6 as an explicit entry in §2 and §4.5:
> 
> | SRv6 (IPv6 RH type 4) | The outer Routing Extension Header (NH=43) is not walked by the current implementation. SRv6 inner peek is out of scope for this revision; falls back to outer-only hash. |
> 
> This also directly answers the open question raised inline.
> 
> ### 💡 Suggestions
> * §9 Known Issues already tracks the compile-time guard on `flow_hash_config_t` bit overflow — consider adding SRv6 inner peek as a follow-up item there too, so it doesn't get lost.
> 
> ### Status
> ⚠️ Approved with minor suggestions — the SRv6 exclusion should be documented before merge, but no blocking issues.

updated, excluded SRv6 explicitly.

> hi @yejianquan , have you run sonic-mgmt sanity with this change? Please share the test result to make sure no breakage.
Hi @yue-fred-gao , yeah, all of them passed.

sonic-mgmt fib/test_fib.py results
 ====================================================
 PASS    test_basic_fib[True-True-1514]
 PASS    test_hash[ipv4]
 PASS    test_hash[ipv6]
 PASS    test_ipinip_hash[ipv4]
 PASS    test_ipinip_hash[ipv6]
 PASS    test_ipinip_hash_negative[ipv4]
 PASS    test_ipinip_hash_negative[ipv6]
 PASS    test_vxlan_hash[ipv4-ipv4]
 PASS    test_vxlan_hash[ipv6-ipv6]
 PASS    test_vxlan_hash[ipv6-ipv4]
 PASS    test_nvgre_hash[ipv4-ipv4]
 PASS    test_nvgre_hash[ipv4-ipv6]
 PASS    test_nvgre_hash[ipv6-ipv6]
 PASS    test_nvgre_hash[ipv6-ipv4]
 PASS    test_vxlan_hash[ipv4-ipv6]    (body PASS, teardown ERROR*)
 PASS    test_ecmp_group_member_flap[True-False-1514]    (body PASS, teardown ERROR*)
 ====================================================
 16 testcase bodies passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped.

 (*) Both teardown ERRORs are infra-only: loganalyzer's
 parallel_run fixture failed because core_uploader.py could
 not reach the corefile collector proxy (-
 timeout) to upload a pre-existing core file
 syncd.1778467508.68.core dated 2026-04-09 — well before
 this patch was deployed. The patch under test did not
 generate any new core files (no fresh entries in /var/core
 during the 2-hour run). These errors are unrelated to the
 patch and have been seen in prior unrelated runs on
 vlab-vpp-01.

Post-test sanity check: 5 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed (no DUT regressions).

DUT verification artifacts (collected immediately after r13):

 - vppctl show bond: 8 BondEthernets all on l34-inner ✓
 - vppctl show hash: both hash-eth-l34-inner (new) and hash-eth-l34 (legacy, unchanged) registered ✓
 - vppctl show ip fib: peek_inner set on VRF:0 v4 ✓
 - BGP: 24/24 Established ✓
 - No new cores in /var/core/ ✓

@patilshashidhar @yue-fred-gao @dypet — could you please review this fix? Thank you!
@judyjoseph — could you please review this fix as well? Thank you!

> @patilshashidhar @yue-fred-gao @dypet — could you please review this fix? Thank you!

hi @xq9mend , thanks for the contribution. Actually I have never used or paid attention to vppcfgd. What is it for and how to use it? How did you test it? thanks

hi @yue-fred-gao,

`yaml.load()` without an explicit `Loader` supports Python-specific tags and can deserialize non-standard objects. `yaml.SafeLoader` restricts deserialization to standard YAML types (strings, numbers, lists, dicts), which is the safe and recommended choice for parsing config files. See [PyYAML docs](https://pyyaml.org/wiki/PyYAMLDocumentation) and [CVE-2017-18342](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18342) for background.

There are no unit tests for `vppcfgd` in this repo. The fix is a one-line change; I verified it produces identical output on a sample `constants.yml`.
my point is it is not used and probably should be removed.
That's a valid point. Removal would be outside the scope of this security fix — keeping this change minimal and focused.

Hi @yue-fred-gao, could you please help review again?

Few updates:
1. APPL_DB schema is added
2. dedup process is added to solve the VPP tunnel duplication due to SONiC programs encap/decap tunnels with the same src/dst IP.
3. add section to describe the solution to fix the tunnel unnumber failure due to rif not ready.

As for this PR: https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-vpp/pull/221, can we do this in two step, first let's unblock the SONiC decap with this hacky solution, and add a new ipip tunnel flag to support this afterwards?

> Hi @yue-fred-gao, could you please help review again?
> 
> Few updates:
> 
> 1. APPL_DB schema is added
> 2. dedup process is added to solve the VPP tunnel duplication due to SONiC programs encap/decap tunnels with the same src/dst IP.
> 3. add section to describe the solution to fix the tunnel unnumber failure due to rif not ready.
> 
> As for this PR: #221, can we do this in two step, first let's unblock the SONiC decap with this hacky solution, and add a new ipip tunnel flag to support this afterwards?

Thanks for the update. I think new ipip tunnel flag is not very complicated so I prefer you use the same commit to complete it. The reason is that for each new update to vpp (new patch or new version), we need to upload the prebuilt debs to buildkite so they are not rebuilt again. We want to minimize the versions in buildkite to limit the space usage.

> > Hi @yue-fred-gao, could you please help review again?
> > Few updates:
> > 
> > 1. APPL_DB schema is added
> > 2. dedup process is added to solve the VPP tunnel duplication due to SONiC programs encap/decap tunnels with the same src/dst IP.
> > 3. add section to describe the solution to fix the tunnel unnumber failure due to rif not ready.
> > 
> > As for this PR: #221, can we do this in two step, first let's unblock the SONiC decap with this hacky solution, and add a new ipip tunnel flag to support this afterwards?
> 
> Thanks for the update. I think new ipip tunnel flag is not very complicated so I prefer you use the same commit to complete it. The reason is that for each new update to vpp (new patch or new version), we need to upload the prebuilt debs to buildkite so they are not rebuilt again. We want to minimize the versions in buildkite to limit the space usage.

Hi @yue-fred-gao, thanks, one question: we only patches the downstream version, right? why do we need to upload debs to buildkite?

> > > Hi @yue-fred-gao, could you please help review again?
> > > Few updates:
> > > 
> > > 1. APPL_DB schema is added
> > > 2. dedup process is added to solve the VPP tunnel duplication due to SONiC programs encap/decap tunnels with the same src/dst IP.
> > > 3. add section to describe the solution to fix the tunnel unnumber failure due to rif not ready.
> > > 
> > > As for this PR: #221, can we do this in two step, first let's unblock the SONiC decap with this hacky solution, and add a new ipip tunnel flag to support this afterwards?
> > 
> > 
> > Thanks for the update. I think new ipip tunnel flag is not very complicated so I prefer you use the same commit to complete it. The reason is that for each new update to vpp (new patch or new version), we need to upload the prebuilt debs to buildkite so they are not rebuilt again. We want to minimize the versions in buildkite to limit the space usage.
> 
> Hi @yue-fred-gao, thanks, one question: we only patches the downstream version, right? why do we need to upload debs to buildkite?
@lolyu , we upload debs to buildkite to avoid building vpp from source every time. It saves CPU resources and make the sonic-vpp build more stable.
